name: Bossie asks for the user's name on the very first message
description: Brand-new account with user.name unset. The first text message Bossie receives should be answered with a one-line ask for name + business, NOT routed to the LLM. The user's reply ('Diego, Riley Roofing Co.') is parsed and persisted via PATCH /me + PUT /profile/identity. Subsequent turns use the captured name in the topbar greeting and sidebar footer.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.role: Owner-operator, fence + deck contractor, just verified phone
persona.context: First time in the app. Has only entered a phone number — nothing else.

steps.expect: Assistant replies in <1s with: 'Hey 👋 quick one before we start — what should I call you? (And what's your business name, if it's different?)'. No LLM round-trip happens.
Assistant: 'Got it, Diego — what can I help you with?'; user.name === 'Diego'; businessIdentity.businessName === 'Riley Roofing Co.'
Topbar greeting reads 'Hey, Diego 👋'; sidebar footer shows DR avatar + 'Diego' + 'Riley Roofing Co.'
Account card shows Name=Diego; Business identity card shows Display name=Riley Roofing Co.

evaluationCriteria.check: Onboarding ask uses plain assistant bubble — no special wizard treatment, just a friendly text turn
Reply round-trip <300ms because no LLM call happens; the ack feels instant
extractNameAndBusiness correctly parses the comma-separated form; trailing 'Co.' period is preserved
Ask is in chat live region; screen reader announces it like any other assistant turn
